title: Transfusion Transmitted Injuries Surveillance System, 2020 to 2021: Infographic - Canada.ca
content:
## Transfusion Transmitted Injuries Surveillance System, 2020 to 2021: Infographic
Download the alternative format (PDF format, 272 KB, 1 page)
- Organization: Public Health Agency of Canada
- Date published: 2023-12-XX
The Transfusion Transmitted Injuries Surveillance System (TTISS) network monitors over 95% of transfusion activities in Canadian hospitals. This is a summary of the 2020 to 2021 data:
- A total of 1,765 adverse transfusion events (ATEs) Footnote 1 were reported, with 897 cases in 2020 and 868 cases in 2021
- 65% (n=1,147/1,765) of ATEs were caused by transfusion of blood components and 35% (n=618/1,765) of ATEs were caused by transfusion of plasma derivatives
- 4.9% (n=87/1,765) of ATEs resulted in life-threatening injuries
- 1.1% (n=19/1,765) of ATEs resulted in transfusion-related deaths
- 8 deaths occurred per million units of blood components transfused
Breakdown of the counts and proportions of reported cases according to ATE types.
- Delayed Haemolytic Reaction (DHR): 3.9%
- Hypotensive Reaction (HYPT): 9.4%
- Intravenous Immune Globulin Headache (IVIG-HD): 11.8%
- Severe Anaphylactic or Anaphylactoid Reaction (SAAR): 10.5%
- Transfusion-Associated Circulatory Overload (TACO): 32.1%
- Transfusion-Associated Dyspnea (TAD): 6.3%
- Transfusion-Related Acute Lung Injury (TRALI): 2.7%

## ATEs by Severity Footnote 2
- Grade 1 (non-severe): 1,199 (67.9%)
- Grade 2 (severe): 453 (25.7%)
- Grade 3 (life-threatening): 87 (4.9%)
- Not determined: 26 (1.5%)
- Minor or no sequelae: 1,589 (90.0%)
- Major or long-term sequelae: 63 (3.6%)
- Death: (transfusion related): 19 (1.1%) (Definite, probable or possible)
- Death: (non-transfusion related): 13 (0.7%) (Doubtful, ruled out or not determined)
- Not determined: 81 (4.6%)

## Deaths Related to ATEs

## ATE and Death Rate per Million Units of Blood Components Transfused
- Units of blood components transfused: 2,327,764
- ATEs related to blood components: 1,147
- ATE rate: 492 / million units of blood components
- ATE risk: 1 case / 2,029 units of blood components
- Deaths related to blood components: 19
- Death rate: 8 / million units of blood components
- Death risk: 1 case / 122,514 units of blood components
Transfusion Transmitted Injuries Surveillance System, User's manual version 3.0 (PDF)
https://ttiss.mcmaster.ca/wp-content/uploads/2016/10/Current-V3-CTAERF-Manual-E-2008-04-15.pdf
Return to footnote 1 referrer
Please refer to the Transfusion Transmitted Injuries Surveillance System 2016-2020 Summary Report for the definitions of "severity", "outcome", and "related death", as well as descriptions of the types of ATEs and results from previous years https://www.canada.ca/en/public-health/services/publications/drugs-health-products/transfusion-transmitted-injuries-surveillance-system-summary-report-2016-2020.html
Return to footnote 2 referrer
